items.title: Computer vision with limited labels
items.description: Medical imaging, retail SKU photos, and satellite tiles often have hundreds—not millions—of labels. Standard flips, crops, and color jitter are default regularizers before collecting more annotations.

items.title: NLP on small labeled corpora
items.description: Intent classification and sentiment models on a few thousand utterances use back-translation or synonym replacement to reduce overfitting to exact phrasing in the training set.

items.title: Imbalanced tabular fraud or churn
items.description: SMOTE or related oversamplers balance minority classes so classifiers do not overfit the majority label; paired with proper hold-out evaluation on real rows only.

items.title: Fine-tuning pretrained vision and language models
items.description: Small domain-specific fine-tune sets overfit quickly; augmentation during fine-tuning (image transforms or text paraphrase) stabilizes validation metrics on niche datasets.

items.title: Edge deployment with camera variation
items.description: Training-time jitter for exposure, blur, and crop simulates phone angles and lighting so models generalize to field captures without retraining on every device.
